title: Failed to add inotify watch + Failed to open cgroups file
body:
### Issue Description

This is an example of the Jellyfin container, but I also have this with others using a fswatch (I assume they do):

```bash
apr 04 13:11:14 core conmon[7752]: conmon ... <nwarn>: Failed to add inotify watch for /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/jellyfin.service/libpod-payload-.../memory.events
apr 04 13:11:14 core conmon[7752]: conmon ... <nwarn>: Failed to open cgroups file: /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/jellyfin.service/libpod-payload-.../memory.events
```
This is on latest OpenSUSE MicroOS + Aeon. Could this be an SELinux issue?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start (user) container with filesystem watcher
2. Open journalctl
3. Flood with `Failed to add inotify watch` and `Failed to open cgroups file`


### Describe the results you received

```bash
apr 04 13:13:18 core conmon[8072]: conmon ... <nwarn>: Failed to add inotify watch for /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/jellyfin.service/libpod-payload-.../memory.events
apr 04 13:13:18 core conmon[8072]: conmon ... <nwarn>: Failed to open cgroups file: /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/jellyfin.service/libpod-payload-.../memory.events
```

### Describe the results you expected

Working fswatch + cgroups error gone.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

OpenSUSE MicroOS with latest updates

### Additional information

This seems to be related <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24942>:

```bash

Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
KillMode=mixed
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm -v -f -i --cidfile=%t/%N.cid
Delegate=yes
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
SyslogIdentifier=%N
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --log-driver none --cgroups=split --network systemd-media --sdnotify=conmo>

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

comments.body:
Bug has been reported upstream: https://bugzilla.suse.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1241121

It seems something is blocking or missing. Hopefully it can be solved, because it seems apps like Jellyfin seem to have difficulty picking up file changes.

I am also seeing this on Podman in Rocky 8. Not using SELinux either. Unfortunately, I think this is the latest Rocky 8 version. In case it matters, I am also using `crun` instead of `runc`
```text
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.9.4-rhel
API Version:  4.9.4-rhel
Go Version:   go1.22.11 (Red Hat 1.22.11-1.module+el8.10.0+1936+ea614e46)
Built:        Wed Mar 26 16:50:46 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
and
```text
# conmon --version
conmon version 2.1.10
commit: 267637e798c683580abd360ac57435c36c11a2a3
```
I've tried adjusting 
```text
sysctr -w fs.inotify.max_user_instances=256
```
As well as other higher values and `fs.inotify.max_user_watches` as well thinking that might help but it didn't.

I am also facing same issue while trying to install Elasticsearch as podman container on RHEL 8.

`Failed to add inotify watch for /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/elasticsearch-test.service/libpod-payload-****/memory.events
Failed to open cgroups file: /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1002.slice/user@1002.service/elasticsearch-test.service/libpod-payload-****/memory.events`

Try to create a File `/etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d/delegate.conf` with the Content
```
[Service]
Delegate=memory
```

and restart

I just ran into this exact issue today with rootless quadlets. This absolutely depends on workload, because I have traefik running with systemd sockets in the same rootless environment just fine, but `copyparty` periodically fails with this error, causing the service to restart.

For me this is also on opensuse tumbleweed, with selinux enforcing. I don't see any `avc` errors logged though, so I don't believe its due to that.
I've stopped using OpenSUSE, and moved to Fedora.

I have this issue with rootless quadlets on Dietpi using a klutchell's unbound container (latest). Tried CgroupsMode=no-conmon with no success. Interesting that just like OP I have also jellyfin running but it didn't provoke these errors. I would like to see this issue reopened, seems like it is recent and is not distribution-specific. No apparmor or selinux.

k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: {} store: configFile: /home/dock/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 6 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 6 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/dock/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 235838849024 graphRootUsed: 9785327616 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Supports shifting: "false" Supports volatile: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 10 runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers transientStore: false volumePath: /home/dock/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 5.4.2 BuildOrigin: Debian Built: 1753479578 BuiltTime: Fri Jul 25 18:39:38 2025 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.24.4 Os: linux OsArch: linux/arm64 Version: 5.4.2 ``` I also think this is not a distro related issue, both issues regarding this topic have been closed because it appears is not something of high severity, but is an inconvenient amount of irrelevant messages in system logs, especially for space in the disk and debugging. Below I share an screenshot of the situation:

Well, I've solved it. The problem is with the file memory.events, which is provided by the memory cgroup controller. You have to make sure that controller is available for your user. In my case, it was even disabled for root in the cmdline arguments for the kernel. I enabled that, and now it's working.

Sorry I don't provide a more in-depth explanation, but I hope this helps orient the research of people with this problem.
Below I leave some links that may be useful:

https://www.hackerstack.org/understanding-linux-cgroups/
https://github.com/raspberrypi/linux/issues/6980
https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Cgroups

Please forgive if I've miss-understood. OP is on OpenSUSE, can this be fixed with...

```
$ cat /etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d/60-delegate.conf
[Service]
Delegate=pids memory
```

I assume this is fairly standard across distros. 

REF: https://doc.opensuse.org/documentation/leap/tuning/html/book-tuning/cha-tuning-cgroups.html#sec-cgroups-user-sessions
